block_01: Trust your gut or trust your chart?
block_02: Most traders follow one of two trading styles:
block_03: Objective traders rely solely on analysis. Decisions are made based on the established rules.
block_04: Subjective traders can change the rules as they see fit and base their trading decisions on their judgment about a particular trading opportunity.
block_07: Defined entry, exit, time frames, order types, and markets
block_09: Lower risks and less emotional involvement. Suits beginner traders.
block_15: Important point: subjective trading ≠ trading on the “feel”. Such “intuition” is the result of years of market practice. For example, an experienced trader can assess whether a market move is just a temporary corrective action or an actual trend reversal.
block_17: In any case, both groups of traders DO use technical and fundamental analysis, just with a different degree of creativity involved.
block_18: Here are a few tools to help your trading decisions on the CapitalBear platform, regardless of your trading style.
block_20: Technical indicators help traders better understand the market and make decisions based on reason, not intuition. The CapitalBear platform features 100+ trend, momentum, volume, and volatility indicators — apply them to your charts and follow the signals!
block_23: This is a platform widget that shows the current Buy/Sell ratio for a chosen asset. For example, if the widget is 70% red, it means that 70% of traders clicked “Sell” and only 30% chose to buy.
block_26: Context is crucial in trading — it means focusing on the big picture instead of the short-term changes. This is what fundamental analysis does. Stay on top of things — start your day with market news in the Market Analysis section.
block_28:
